---
title: "Development of language networks"
---
::: {.callout-tip collapse=true}
# Learning Goals: Development of language networks

**Claim-evidence mapping** 

1. Describe evidence for the following claims:
   a) Children’s language networks develop gradually
   b) Language networks are profoundly altered in Deaf people who were not exposed to a signed language in early childhood
   c) In survivors of left-hemisphere perinatal stroke, language is processed by the brain regions in the right hemisphere that correspond to the typical language areas in the left hemisphere
   e) In multilinguals, the neural organization of language is affected by both (i) the age at which the person began acquiring the language, and (2) the person's proficiency level in the language

2. Given a new piece of evidence, explain what it suggests about the aspects of the language network that are already established in infancy

:::

| Date | Topic | Reading | Slides |
| - | --- | --- | - |
| Tues 10/20 | Children's language networks | Newport & Getz 2025 |
| Thurs 10/22 | Critical periods, language deprivation | 
| Tues 10/27 | Perinatal stroke | Newport et al. 2022 | 
| Thurs 10/29 | Bilingualism | 
| Tues 11/3 | Bilingualism | Wartenburger et al. 2003
| Thurs 11/5 |  <span class="exam-badge">Exam #3</span>


**Resources**

* [BrainFacts.org: Critical periods](https://www.brainfacts.org/archives/2012/critical-periods)
* [PBS: Deaf advocates say learning sign language early is crucial](https://www.pbs.org/video/deaf-learning-1687030137/)
* [Stroke.org: Perinatal stroke](https://www.stroke.org.uk/stroke/childhood/stroke-unborn-newborn-babies-perinatal-stroke)
